findings.evidence: Lines 154-158 (`counit_includeRight`), 163-178 (`comul_includeRight`), 194-198 (`antipode_includeRight`), 221-224 (`map_includeRight`) all index their LHS on `includeRight`; bottom-up simp rewrites that subterm first via `includeRight_apply`, then none of those four patterns match.
findings.fix: Remove `@[simp]` from `includeRight_apply`. If callers need to unfold `includeRight` they can use `simp only [includeRight_apply]` or `rw`; the characteristic lemmas should be the normal-form rewriters.
findings.issue: `@[simp]` on `includeRight_apply` (proof `rfl`) causes simp to rewrite `includeRight a` → `Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight a` before outer lemmas fire; `counit_includeRight`, `comul_includeRight`, `antipode_includeRight`, and `map_includeRight` all have `includeRight` in their LHS and become dead simp lemmas whenever `includeRight_apply` pre-fires on the subterm.

summary: `includeRight_apply` is annotated `@[simp]` with proof `rfl`, which causes simp to eagerly unfold `includeRight` to `Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight` when processing subterms bottom-up; this fires before `counit_includeRight`, `comul_includeRight`, `antipode_includeRight`, and `map_includeRight`, making those four characteristic simp lemmas unreachable in typical `simp` calls. Otherwise the surface is minimal, helpers are private, and the characteristic API is complete.
